parsed_review.summary: CP00-635 (RP20.P06.M06.S01-S10, Risk A, 10 units, Synthetic Fixture Set) is a descriptor-only, synthetic-only pack whose pack-level production_ready claim is supported by independent read-only inspection. The contract descriptor (contracts/data-room-vdr-core-contract.json: cp635_p06_synthetic_fixture_head_descriptor) is deep-equal verified against the code-generated descriptor by both the validator script and a dedicated test, giving machine-enforced contract/code parity. The 10 rows match the manifest titles exactly and resolve to the declared 8 implementation + 2 security_audit distribution (Permission matrix row, Audit hint fields), with micro-title Synthetic Fixture Set x10. No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are attested in the contract and enforced by validator assertions; deny-over-allow is enforced (deny_over_allow_enforced true, permission_bypass_detected false) and no-leak guards (tenant scope, matter trace, object-storage/audit-body/permission-decision-detail exclusions) are present. The authority boundary is correct: Claude is not the final approver, local validation does not claim enterprise trust, and runtime opening requires human approval (deferred to CP00-636). Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor/receipt shape (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false), not product runtime. command-evidence.json records all expected validation commands plus npm test/build/git diff --check at exit 0, and the readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON; no auth failure, 0-byte, or malformed-output evidence). Plan live_state and next-pack-queue both pin CP00-635 with next unit RP20.P06.M06.S11 / next pack CP00-636, and the README CP00-635 entry is accurate. The manifest, construction-inspection, and adjudication correctly remain production_ready=false/pending pre-review rather than prematurely claiming readiness. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
